attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 200m or less, relying on superior timing and top-end speed rather than early positioning. Does not attack; contests flat bunch sprints exclusively.
team_tactics: Heavily dependent on Quick-Step/Deceuninck three-man leadout train (Asgreen-Lampaert-Morkov pattern). When leadout collapses or teammates crash, shows positioning vulnerability and often gets boxed. Team also uses him as diesel engine for echelon work.
terrain_preference: Pure flat bunch sprints on technical finishes where late acceleration compensates for positioning errors. Severely exposed on any gradient finish, false flats, or categorized climbs—dropped repeatedly with 40-70km remaining on even moderate climbs.
vulnerability: Dropped on any sustained gradient (even false flats). Positioning errors in chaotic finales without leadout. Form decline post-2019 evident with hesitant sprinting and inability to win without perfect setup. Crashes/mechanical disruptions prove insurmountable.
key_patterns: Dropped on climbs with 40-70km remaining in 6+ instances, requiring chase efforts
Won from terrible positions (25th at 800m) when leadout failed, showing exceptional top speed
Deliberate wheel-hopping abandoning own leadout when lacking confidence, seen repeatedly 2019 Giro
Perfect timing <200m to go when positioned correctly, but premature launches when nervous
Quick-Step three-man train essential: multiple positioning crises when train collapses or crashes
Clear form decline 2020-2021: hesitant checking shoulders, barely top-10 finishes, dropped repeatedly
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline from 2019 peak through 2021 with hesitant sprinting, repeated drops on minor climbs, inability to contest without perfect setup, and barely top-10 finishes replacing podiums
